task: Improve the Study discussion guides page using GOV.UK Frontend and Nunjucks, then open a new pull request. Follow up by handling the unresolved Codex review thread on PR #358 and fixing failing checks.

reviewCommentHandling.issue: The variables drawer opened below the 34rem editor/preview area without reliably revealing the panel or focusing a useful control.
reviewCommentHandling.resolution: Drawer containers are focusable, opening a drawer scrolls it into view, focus moves to the preferred drawer control when available, and the controller boots even when the route loader reaches it after DOMContentLoaded.
reviewCommentHandling.procedure: After pushing the fix, add a thumbs-up reaction to the original comment, reply directly with fix and validation evidence, and resolve the thread.

ciFailureInvestigation: CI and Validate ResearchOps failed at lint because src/styles/guides.scss did not pass Prettier.
Worker CI published the same Prettier patch for src/styles/guides.scss.
Release Gate failed because blocking lint and format checks failed; npm audit advisories were reported as non-blocking by repository policy.

validation.command: Browser alignment check at http://127.0.0.1:8891/pages/study/guides/?id=RECT3O7DT with viewport 1212x672
validation.result: passed; preview panel top and guide source textarea top both measured at 1181px

validation.command: Browser interaction check at http://127.0.0.1:8891/pages/study/guides/?id=RECT3O7DT: click Variables
validation.result: passed; drawer was visible in the 672px viewport with top 107px and focus on drawer-variables-close

validation.command: npx prettier -c public/js/guides-route-loader.js public/components/guides/guides-page.js tests/study-child-route-state.test.js tests/study-guides-route-state.test.js src/styles/guides.scss docs/agent-audit/reasoning/2026/06/05/study-guides-govuk-polish.md docs/agent-audit/reasoning/2026/06/05/study-guides-govuk-polish.json

residualRisk: No known residual implementation risk after validation. Existing dependency audit advisories remain outside this PR and are classified as non-blocking by the repository release-gate policy.
